proofs.proof_tex: Proof. Write f_n(o)=chi_P(o) B(o)^(-1/2) 1{n^(-1)<B(o)<=bar_b}. By def:boundary-score and def:shell-summand, S_nP=rho_n(alpha) n^(-1) sum_{i=1}^n {f_n(O_i)-P f_n}. By ass:iid, Var_P(S_nP)=rho_n(alpha)^2 n^(-1) Var_P(f_n(O)). By def:alpha-normalization this equals Var_P(f_n)/K_alpha(n^(-1)). Applying ass:centered-shell-variance at t=n^(-1) gives c_V_minus <= Var_P(S_nP) <= c_V_plus for all large n with n^(-1)<=bar_b. Thus take c_1=c_V_minus and c_2=c_V_plus. If a different deterministic normalization a_n were used in place of rho_n(alpha), the same computation would multiply the variance by (a_n/rho_n(alpha))^2; hence nondegeneracy bounded away from both zero and infinity is possible exactly when a_n is of the same order as rho_n(alpha). Finally def:tail-scale gives K_alpha(n^(-1))=1 for alpha>1, K_alpha(n^(-1))=1+log n for alpha=1, and K_alpha(n^(-1))=n^(1-alpha) for 0<alpha<1. Therefore rho_n(alpha) is respectively of order n^(1/2), (n/log n)^(1/2), and n^(alpha/2).

Proof. Fix u_0 in (0,1]. For finite u_1,...,u_m in [u_0,1], H_nP_u0(u_j)=sum_{i=1}^n X_{n,i,j} with X_{n,i,j}=n^(-1){G_n,P,u_j(O_i)-P G_n,P,u_j}. In the branch alpha>=1, lem:gaussian-shell-fidi applies ass:shell-row-lindeberg and gives convergence of every such vector to a centered Gaussian vector with covariance Sigma_P,u0(u_j,u_k). In the branch 0<alpha<1, lem:stable-shell-fidi applies ass:stable-shell-domain and ass:stable-shell-drift to the joint row array containing J_n,P and the G_n,P,u_j coordinates; hence the finite-dimensional laws converge to the infinitely divisible law with Levy measure Lambda_P,alpha,u0, and H_P,alpha,u0 is its truncated-shell marginal. Tightness of H_nP_u0 in D([u_0,1]) follows from lem:truncated-process-tightness, which is exactly the moment-modulus condition ass:shell-increment-moment plus the standard tightness criterion. Combining finite-dimensional convergence and tightness gives weak convergence in D([u_0,1]). Nondegeneracy follows from lem:local-limit-nondegeneracy: for alpha>=1 the variance at u=1 is bounded away from zero by ass:centered-shell-variance, while for 0<alpha<1 the jump projection is nonzero by ass:stable-shell-nondegenerate-jump. When alpha>1 the limiting process is the mean-zero Gaussian process indexed by Sigma_P,u0. When alpha=1 the same proof gives the Gaussian finite-dimensional law on every fixed [u_0,1], but lem:alpha-one-no-zero-extension, using ass:boundary-annulus-oscillation, rules out an extension to u_0=0 with the same normalization and a right-continuous/tight origin. When 0<alpha<1, the preceding infinitely divisible convergence identifies the joint law of the extreme-shell coordinate and the truncated-shell vector, and the asserted H_P,alpha,u0 is its truncated-shell marginal.

Proof. The fixed-u_0 assertion is exactly the tightness part of thm:local-experiment. For alpha=1, lem:alpha-one-no-zero-extension gives constants eta,epsilon>0 and arbitrarily small annuli on which limsup_n P_P(sup_{u,v in (0,delta]} |H_n(u)-H_n(v)|>eta)>=epsilon. Taking the limit delta downarrow 0 proves the displayed failure of uniform asymptotic equicontinuity at the boundary point u=0. For 0<alpha<1, lem:extreme-shell-first-order applies the joint infinitely divisible convergence and ass:stable-shell-nondegenerate-jump to the first, extreme-shell coordinate. It yields R_nP => R_P with a nondegenerate infinitely divisible law, so R_nP is not o_P(1). This is precisely the claimed nonuniform phenomenon: the fixed truncation H_nP_u0 is tight for every u_0>0, but the omitted shell 0<B<=n^(-1) remains first order in the joint extreme-shell/truncated-shell decomposition. The statement makes no additional scalar-endpoint or full-domain ell_infinity claim beyond these two consequences.

Proof. First apply lem:ccot-vonmises-expansion-from-delta. Since theta_L=Gamma(P) and hat_theta_L_n=Gamma(hat_P_n), it gives rho_n(alpha){hat_theta_L_n-theta_L}=rho_n(alpha)(hat_P_n-P)phi_P+rho_n(alpha)(hat_P_n-P){chi_P B^(-1/2)1{0<B<=bar_b}}+o_P(1). Under ass:boundary-atom-free the boundary indicator decomposes without an atom term into 0<B<=n^(-1) and n^(-1)<B<=bar_b. By def:boundary-score and def:extreme-shell-residual, the second empirical term is S_nP+R_nP. This proves the displayed decomposition. If alpha>1, def:tail-scale gives rho_n(alpha)=n^(1/2). Lem:extreme-shell-negligible gives R_nP=o_P(1), and lem:regular-boundary-gaussian-sum applies ass:regular-boundary-joint-limit to the joint row array of n^(-1/2)phi_P and the boundary score. Hence the scalar endpoint limit is Gaussian with the stated regular-boundary cross-covariance. If alpha=1, lem:extreme-shell-negligible again gives R_nP=o_P(1), while lem:regular-term-negligible-under-boundary-scaling gives rho_n(alpha)n^(-1)sum_i phi_P(O_i)=o_P(1). The remaining shell term has the logarithmic Gaussian transition law supplied by thm:local-experiment on every fixed truncated domain. If 0<alpha<1, lem:regular-term-negligible-under-boundary-scaling removes the regular term, and lem:stable-shell-fidi gives joint convergence of (R_nP,H_nP_u0) to the infinitely divisible law indexed by Lambda_P,alpha,u0. Lem:irregular-endpoint-projection-limit then applies ass:irregular-endpoint-projection-nondegeneracy to the scalar projection R+H(1), showing that the scalar endpoint limit is nondegenerate and non-Gaussian. This limit-law contribution is distinct from the continuous-instrument CCOT PRTE rate in TanBlanchetSyrgkanis2026PRTEOT and from regularized OT partial-ID inference: here the active-face OT shell derivative and the explicit extreme-shell residual determine the unregularized sharp-bound endpoint law.

Proof. This is a conditional fixed-alpha result; the open object is the construction of a calibrator satisfying ass:fixed-alpha-calibrator from primitive estimators. Conditional on that assumption, def:alpha-specific-ci-handle defines T_n,alpha=rho_n(alpha)(hat_theta_L_n-theta_L), generalized quantiles hat q_-(alpha), hat q_+(alpha), and C_n_alpha=[hat_theta_L_n-rho_n(alpha)^(-1)hat q_+(alpha),hat_theta_L_n-rho_n(alpha)^(-1)hat q_-(alpha)]. The event theta_L in C_n_alpha is exactly {hat q_-(alpha)<=T_n,alpha<=hat q_+(alpha)}. Lem:fixed-alpha-ci-coverage uses ass:fixed-alpha-calibrator to show that this event has asymptotic probability at least 1-delta uniformly over P in P_n(alpha), including the alpha=1 transition because the calibrated law is the scalar logarithmic transition law from thm:endpoint-limit-law. Lem:fixed-alpha-ci-length gives |C_n_alpha|=rho_n(alpha)^(-1){hat q_+(alpha)-hat q_-(alpha)} and a uniform expected length O(rho_n(alpha)^(-1)). Combining the two lemmas proves the claimed conditional construction.

added_lemmas.statement: For a triangular array of independent mean-zero R^m-valued row variables whose row-sum covariance matrices converge to Sigma and whose array satisfies the multivariate Lindeberg condition, the row sums converge in distribution to N(0,Sigma).

source.locator: Theorem 2.27, multivariate Lindeberg central limit theorem

added_lemmas.proof_tex: Let X_{n,1},...,X_{n,k_n} be independent mean-zero random vectors in R^m. If sum_i Cov(X_{n,i}) -> Sigma and, for every epsilon>0, sum_i E[||X_{n,i}||^2 1{||X_{n,i}||>epsilon}] -> 0, then sum_i X_{n,i} converges weakly to the centered normal law N_m(0,Sigma).

added_lemmas.statement: A sequence of processes on a compact interval with uniformly bounded q-th moment at one point and increment bound E|X_n(t)-X_n(s)|^q <= C|t-s|^beta for q>0 and beta>1 is tight in D on that interval, and every subsequential limit has a continuous modification.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: If for some alpha>0, beta>1 and nondecreasing continuous F one has E|X_n(t)-X_n(s)|^alpha <= |F(t)-F(s)|^beta uniformly in n, together with tightness of X_n(t_0), then the laws of X_n are tight in D, and the modulus of continuity is tight.

added_lemmas.statement: For a triangular array of independent infinitesimal row variables in R^m, vague convergence of n times the row law on sets bounded away from zero, convergence of compensated drift terms for a common truncation function, and negligible Gaussian part imply convergence of the row sum to the infinitely divisible law with the stated Levy measure and drift.

source.locator: Theorem 15.14, convergence of infinitesimal triangular arrays

added_lemmas.proof_tex: For an infinitesimal triangular array, convergence of the Levy measures on continuity sets bounded away from zero, convergence of the truncated first moments, and convergence of the small-jump covariance term characterize weak convergence of the row sums to the infinitely divisible distribution with that Levy triplet.

added_lemmas.statement: If Gamma is Hadamard differentiable at P tangentially to the empirical-process tangent set with derivative dGamma_P, and the empirical process sqrt(n)(hat_P_n-P) is asymptotically tight and tangential, then Gamma(hat_P_n)-Gamma(P)=dGamma_P(hat_P_n-P)+o_P(n^(-1/2)). The same remainder is o_P(rho_n(alpha)^(-1)) for every rho_n(alpha)<=sqrt(n).
added_lemmas.proof_tex: If phi is Hadamard differentiable at theta tangentially to T and r_n(theta_n-theta) converges weakly to an element of T, then r_n(phi(theta_n)-phi(theta)) converges weakly to phi'_theta applied to the limit; equivalently phi(theta_n)-phi(theta)=phi'_theta(theta_n-theta)+o_P(r_n^(-1)) along the empirical path.

added_lemmas.statement: The normalization rho_n(alpha)=sqrt(n/K_alpha(n^(-1))) has order n^(1/2), (n/log n)^(1/2), and n^(alpha/2) in the regimes alpha>1, alpha=1, and 0<alpha<1, respectively.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: By def:tail-scale, K_alpha(n^(-1)) equals 1 for alpha>1, 1+log n for alpha=1, and (n^(-1))^(alpha-1)=n^(1-alpha) for 0<alpha<1. Substitution into def:alpha-normalization gives the three orders immediately.

added_lemmas.statement: Under ass:iid and ass:shell-row-lindeberg, for alpha>=1 and fixed u_0 in (0,1], every finite vector (H_nP_u0(u_1),...,H_nP_u0(u_m)) with u_j in [u_0,1] converges to a centered Gaussian vector with covariance Sigma_P,u0(u_j,u_k).
added_lemmas.proof_tex: For fixed u_1,...,u_m, def:truncated-boundary-process writes the vector as sum_{i=1}^n X_{n,i}, where X_{n,i,j}=n^(-1){G_n,P,u_j(O_i)-P G_n,P,u_j}. The variables are independent and mean zero by ass:iid. Ass:shell-row-lindeberg states exactly that this row array satisfies multivariate Lindeberg and that sum_i Cov(X_{n,i,j},X_{n,i,k})=n^(-1)Cov_P(G_n,P,u_j,G_n,P,u_k) converges to Sigma_P,u0(u_j,u_k). Lem:lindeberg-feller-vector-array gives the claimed Gaussian finite-dimensional convergence.

added_lemmas.statement: For 0<alpha<1, under ass:iid, ass:stable-shell-domain, and ass:stable-shell-drift, the joint row sums (R_nP,H_nP_u0(u_1),...,H_nP_u0(u_m)) converge to the infinitely divisible law with Levy measure Lambda_P,alpha,u0 and the corresponding compensated drift.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: By def:extreme-shell-residual and def:truncated-boundary-process, the joint vector is the centered row sum of n^(-1)(J_n,P,G_n,P,u_1,...,G_n,P,u_m). Ass:stable-shell-domain gives vague convergence of n times the row law on sets bounded away from zero to Lambda_P,alpha,u0 and negligible Gaussian part. Ass:stable-shell-drift gives convergence of the compensated drift terms for a common truncation function. With ass:iid, the row variables are independent and identically distributed within each row, so lem:triangular-array-id-convergence applies and yields the stated infinitely divisible finite-dimensional limit.

added_lemmas.statement: Under ass:shell-increment-moment, H_nP_u0 is tight in D([u_0,1]) for every fixed u_0 in (0,1].
added_lemmas.proof_tex: Ass:shell-increment-moment gives q>2, beta>1, and C<infinity such that sup_n E|H_nP_u0(v)-H_nP_u0(u)|^q <= C|v-u|^beta for all u,v in [u_0,1], and sup_n E|H_nP_u0(u_0)|^q <= C. The one-point laws are therefore tight by Markov's inequality, and the increment display is the hypothesis of lem:kolmogorov-tightness-criterion. Hence the laws of H_nP_u0 are tight in D([u_0,1]).

added_lemmas.statement: The local limit experiment in thm:local-experiment is nondegenerate: for alpha>=1 the Gaussian limit has a coordinate with positive variance, and for 0<alpha<1 the jump limit has a nonzero extreme-shell projection.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: For alpha>=1, the coordinate u=1 equals the boundary score S_nP by def:boundary-score and def:truncated-boundary-process. The variance bounds in ass:centered-shell-variance, applied at t=n^(-1), imply liminf_n Var_P(S_nP)>0 after the normalization used in S_nP. Therefore the Gaussian finite-dimensional limit from lem:gaussian-shell-fidi has a nonzero variance coordinate. For 0<alpha<1, lem:stable-shell-fidi identifies the joint infinitely divisible law, and ass:stable-shell-nondegenerate-jump states that the projection of Lambda_P,alpha,u0 onto the extreme-shell coordinate is nonzero. A nonzero Levy projection gives a nondegenerate coordinate, so the local experiment is nondegenerate.

added_lemmas.statement: When alpha=1, ass:boundary-annulus-oscillation implies that the fixed-truncation Gaussian shell processes cannot be extended to u_0=0 with the same normalization as a tight right-continuous boundary process.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: Ass:boundary-annulus-oscillation states that for every sufficiently small delta_0 there are u_n,v_n in (0,delta_0] and eta,epsilon>0 such that liminf_n P_P(|H_nP_delta0(u_n)-H_nP_delta0(v_n)|>eta)>=epsilon. If a tight right-continuous extension to u=0 with the same normalization existed, then the usual modulus condition at the endpoint would imply lim_{delta downarrow 0} limsup_n P_P(sup_{u,v in (0,delta]} |H_n(u)-H_n(v)|>eta)=0 for every eta>0. The displayed oscillation contradicts this condition. Hence no such zero-boundary extension exists.

added_lemmas.statement: For alpha>=1, under ass:iid and ass:extreme-shell-first-moment, R_nP=o_P(1).
added_lemmas.proof_tex: Let h_n(O)=chi_P(O)B(O)^(-1/2)1{0<B(O)<=n^(-1)}. By def:extreme-shell-residual, R_nP=n^(-1)sum_i rho_n(alpha){h_n(O_i)-P h_n}. Therefore E_P|R_nP| <= 2 rho_n(alpha) E_P|h_n(O)|. Ass:extreme-shell-first-moment gives E_P|h_n(O)| <= C n^{-(alpha-1/2)} for all large n. If alpha>1 then rho_n(alpha)=n^(1/2) by def:tail-scale and def:alpha-normalization, so the bound is O(n^(1-alpha))->0. If alpha=1 then rho_n(alpha)=(n/(1+log n))^(1/2), so the bound is O((1+log n)^(-1/2))->0. Markov's inequality gives R_nP=o_P(1).

added_lemmas.statement: If alpha<=1 and ass:regular-influence-centering holds, then rho_n(alpha)n^(-1)sum_{i=1}^n phi_P(O_i)=o_P(1).
added_lemmas.proof_tex: Ass:regular-influence-centering gives E_P phi_P=0 and E_P phi_P^2<infinity. By ass:iid, n^(-1/2)sum_i phi_P(O_i)=O_P(1), for example by Chebyshev's inequality. Thus rho_n(alpha)n^(-1)sum_i phi_P(O_i)=(rho_n(alpha)/sqrt(n)) O_P(1). If alpha=1 then rho_n(alpha)/sqrt(n)=(1+log n)^(-1/2)->0; if 0<alpha<1 then rho_n(alpha)/sqrt(n)=n^((alpha-1)/2)->0. Hence the regular term is o_P(1).

added_lemmas.statement: For alpha>1, under ass:regular-boundary-joint-limit, the sum n^(-1/2)sum_i phi_P(O_i)+S_nP converges to the centered Gaussian law whose variance includes the regular variance, boundary variance, and regular-boundary cross-covariance.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: For alpha>1, def:tail-scale and def:alpha-normalization give rho_n(alpha)=sqrt(n). The statistic n^(-1/2)sum_i phi_P(O_i)+S_nP is a fixed linear combination of the joint triangular row sum formed by n^(-1/2)phi_P(O_i) and n^(-1)(G_n,P,1(O_i)-P G_n,P,1). Ass:regular-boundary-joint-limit states multivariate Lindeberg and convergence of the full covariance matrix, including cross-covariances. Lem:lindeberg-feller-vector-array gives joint Gaussian convergence, and applying the continuous linear map that sums the two coordinates gives the asserted Gaussian scalar limit.

By ass:ccot-gamma-hadamard, the derivative is dGamma_P(h)=int phi_P dh + int chi_P(o)B(o)^(-1/2)1{0<B(o)<=bar_b} dh(o). Ass:ccot-donsker makes the empirical path admissible, so lem:ccot-vonmises-via-delta-method yields Gamma(hat_P_n)-Gamma(P)=(hat_P_n-P)phi_P+(hat_P_n-P)f_B+o_P(n^(-1/2)), where f_B=chi_P B^(-1/2)1{0<B<=bar_b}. Since rho_n(alpha)<=sqrt(n) by def:tail-scale and def:alpha-normalization, multiplying by rho_n(alpha) makes the remainder o_P(1). By ass:boundary-atom-free, f_B splits P-almost surely into chi_P B^(-1/2)1{0<B<=n^(-1)} plus chi_P B^(-1/2)1{n^(-1)<B<=bar_b}. The empirical fluctuation of the first piece multiplied by rho_n(alpha) is R_nP by def:extreme-shell-residual; the second is S_nP by def:boundary-score. This proves the display.

added_lemmas.statement: For 0<alpha<1, the scalar projection R_nP+H_nP_u0(1) converges to a nondegenerate non-Gaussian infinitely divisible law under ass:irregular-endpoint-projection-nondegeneracy.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: Taking u=1 in lem:stable-shell-fidi gives joint convergence of (R_nP,H_nP_u0(1)) to the corresponding two-coordinate infinitely divisible limit. The map (r,h)->r+h is continuous, so the scalar projection converges to the projected infinitely divisible law. Ass:irregular-endpoint-projection-nondegeneracy says that, after deterministic centering, this projected Levy measure is nonzero. A one-dimensional infinitely divisible law with nonzero Levy measure is non-Gaussian and nondegenerate. Therefore R_nP+H_nP_u0(1) has the asserted boundary-irregular scalar limit.

added_lemmas.statement: For 0<alpha<1, under ass:stable-shell-nondegenerate-jump, the extreme-shell statistic R_nP is not o_P(1).
added_lemmas.proof_tex: The first coordinate in lem:stable-shell-fidi is R_nP. Ass:stable-shell-nondegenerate-jump states that the Levy measure projected onto this coordinate is nonzero. Thus R_nP converges along the finite-dimensional limit to a nondegenerate infinitely divisible random variable. A sequence converging to a nondegenerate law cannot converge to zero in probability, so R_nP is not o_P(1).

added_lemmas.statement: Conditional on ass:fixed-alpha-calibrator, the interval C_n_alpha in def:alpha-specific-ci-handle has asymptotic coverage at least 1-delta uniformly over P in P_n(alpha).
added_lemmas.proof_tex: By def:alpha-specific-ci-handle, theta_L in C_n_alpha if and only if hat q_-(alpha)<=T_n,alpha<=hat q_+(alpha), where T_n,alpha=rho_n(alpha)(hat_theta_L_n-theta_L). Ass:fixed-alpha-calibrator states that the estimated distribution hat L_n,alpha uniformly approximates the finite-sample law of T_n,alpha at continuity points of the limiting law and that its delta/2 and 1-delta/2 quantiles are uniformly consistent. Therefore uniformly over P in P_n(alpha), P_P(T_n,alpha<hat q_-(alpha))<=delta/2+o(1) and P_P(T_n,alpha>hat q_+(alpha))<=delta/2+o(1). The union bound gives inf_P P_P(theta_L in C_n_alpha)>=1-delta-o(1). The phase-specific limiting laws whose continuity points are used here are those identified in thm:endpoint-limit-law, including the alpha=1 logarithmic transition law.

added_lemmas.statement: Conditional on ass:fixed-alpha-calibrator, the interval C_n_alpha in def:alpha-specific-ci-handle satisfies sup_{P in P_n(alpha)} E_P|C_n_alpha|=O(rho_n(alpha)^(-1)).
added_lemmas.proof_tex: The interval definition gives |C_n_alpha|=rho_n(alpha)^(-1){hat q_+(alpha)-hat q_-(alpha)}. Ass:fixed-alpha-calibrator includes the uniform bound E_P[hat q_+(alpha)-hat q_-(alpha)]=O(1). Multiplying by rho_n(alpha)^(-1) yields sup_{P in P_n(alpha)} E_P|C_n_alpha|=O(rho_n(alpha)^(-1)).

proposed_definition_changes.current: { P : P satisfies ass:boundary-atom-free, ass:boundary-tail, ass:boundary-mark-stabilization, ass:centered-shell-variance, ass:unique-active-face, ass:dual-gap-scale, ass:regular-linearization, ass:gaussian-shell-lindeberg when alpha >= 1, and ass:stable-shell-domain when 0 < alpha < 1 }
proposed_definition_changes.proposed: { P : P satisfies ass:boundary-atom-free, ass:boundary-tail, ass:boundary-mark-stabilization, ass:centered-shell-variance, ass:unique-active-face, ass:dual-gap-scale, ass:regular-linearization, ass:shell-row-lindeberg when alpha >= 1, and ass:stable-shell-domain when 0 < alpha < 1 }
proposed_definition_changes.reason: The Gaussian shell finite-dimensional convergence is proved at the row-summand scale X_{n,i}=n^(-1)(G_n,P,u(O_i)-P G_n,P,u), so the class must reference ass:shell-row-lindeberg rather than the deprecated n^(-1/2)-scaled shell condition.
